reasoning: The Lean signature encodes exactly the hypotheses and conclusion of the informal statement. `Function.Surjective p`, `Continuous p`, `IsClosedMap p`, `∀ y, IsCompact (p ⁻¹' {y})`, and `CompactSpace Y` correspond precisely to "closed continuous surjective map with compact point-fibers on a compact target", and the conclusion `CompactSpace X` is exactly "X is compact". The only cosmetic issue is that both the closed-map hypothesis and the compact-fiber hypothesis are named `hp` (the first is shadowed), but this affects only tactic-level naming, not the logical content of the theorem statement. Hence the Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
